1.kj: bemoan, flee, get, mourn, make to move, take pity, remove, shake, skip for joy, be sorry, vagabond, way, wandering.

1.dv: a primitive root;  also as denominative from H5331 (נֶצַח)

1.kj: Jehovah, the Lord. Compare H3050 (יָהּ), H3069 (יְהֹוִה).

2.dv: or (2 Chronicles 26:15) יָרָא; a primitive root;

2.gl: properly, an overhanging, i.e. (by implication) an excess, superiority, remainder; also a small rope (as hanging free)
2.kj: [phrase] abundant, cord, exceeding, excellancy(-ent), what they leave, that hath left, plentifully, remnant, residue, rest, string, with.

2.gl: properly, a piercer, i.e. an arrow; by implication, a wound; figuratively, (of God) thunderbolt; the shaft of aspear
2.kj: [phrase] archer, arrow, dart, shaft, staff, wound.
2.dv: from H2686 (חָצַץ); also by interchange for H6086 (עֵץ)

2.gl: to tread; by implication, to walk; also to string abow (by treading on it in bending)
2.kj: archer, bend, come, draw, go (over), guide, lead (forth), thresh, tread (down), walk.

3.gl: to pull down or in pieces, break, destroy
3.kj: beat down, break (down, through), destroy, overthrow, pluck down, pull down, ruin, throw down, [idiom] utterly.

3.gl: to do or make (systematically and habitually), especially to practise

4.gl: the sky (as aloft; the dual perhaps alluding to the visible arch in which the clouds move, as well as to the higher ether where the celestial bodies revolve)
4.dv: dual of an unused singular שָׁמֶה; from an unused root meaning to be lofty;

4.gl: a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity
4.kj: consecrated (thing), dedicated (thing), hallowed (thing), holiness, ([idiom] most) holy ([idiom] day, portion, thing), saint, sanctuary.

4.kj: [idiom] another, [phrase] hypocrite, [phrase] common sort, [idiom] low, man (mean, of low degree), person.
